title: Bridge Essay: Vividness as the similarity between generated imagery and an internal model.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Imaginal** (Phase 10) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Imaginal to mechanistic neuroscience through Vividness, as, the, similarity, between.

**Vividness as the similarity between generated imagery and an internal model.** — Brain Cogn. PMID: 37150045. DOI: 10.1016/j.bandc.2023.105988. Vividness in visual mental imagery has been relatively under-explored compared to imagery'srepresentational format and neural mechanisms. In this paper, we take adeeper look at vividness and suggest that in re-framing it, we can potentially reconcile disparate findings regarding visual cortex activation during imagery. Unlike traditional views of vividness that define the concept in terms of perception, we frame vividness in terms of imagery'srelation to an internal model; the closer the gene
